verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted growth factor (no TM domain) whose dominant fate is ECM deposition as a large latent complex (LLC) via LTBP. However, on activated Tregs, stimulated B cells, platelets, and macrophages, the latent TGF-β1 LAP domain forms covalent disulfide bonds with the TM protein GARP (LRRC32) or LRRC33/NRROS, stably anchoring it to the outer leaflet. This disulfide tethering to a TM scaffold is wash-resistant and cell-state dependent (immune activation), qualifying as stable_surface_attachment. The ECM-deposited pool is secreted_only, but the covalent GARP/LRRC33 surface pool is the biologically relevant surface story for immune-oncology targeting.
